description:
You are working as the company’s new “backup-administrator”.  
    A colleague has already placed three data directories that must be archived every night:

        /home/user/data_alpha/
        /home/user/data_bravo/
        /home/user/data_charlie/

    Goal  
    ----  
    1. Create compressed archives (tar + gzip) of each data_* directory.  
       • All archives must be placed inside  /home/user/archives/ (create it if it does not exist).  
       • Every archive file must follow the EXACT name template  

             <directoryName>_20231001.tar.gz  

         For example, the archive of /home/user/data_alpha/ **must** be called  

             /home/user/archives/data_alpha_20231001.tar.gz  

       • The fixed date string “20231001” is mandatory – assume that today is 2023-10-01, regardless of the actual date in the container.

    2. Produce a human-readable manifest file that summarises the backup run.  
       • The manifest must be created at  

             /home/user/archives/backup_manifest_2023-10-01.log  

       • Its exact text layout (line breaks and spacing) must be:

             ==== DAILY BACKUP MANIFEST 2023-10-01 ==== (3)
             
             <archive_name_1>    <size_bytes_1>    <sha256sum_1>
             <archive_name_2>    <size_bytes_2>    <sha256sum_2>
             <archive_name_3>    <size_bytes_3>    <sha256sum_3>
             
             ==== END OF MANIFEST ====

         Formatting rules:  
         a. The first header line and the footer line must start with four “=” characters, a single space, then the given sentence verbatim.  
         b. Immediately after the header line there must be ONE blank line.  
         c. Each archive line contains three TAB-separated fields in this order:  
              • the file name only (e.g. “data_alpha_20231001.tar.gz”)  
              • the size of the file in **bytes** (`stat -c%s`)  
              • the lowercase hexadecimal SHA-256 checksum (`sha256sum | cut -d' ' -f1`)  
         d. After the last archive line there must again be ONE blank line, followed by the footer and a final newline (manifest ends with “\n”).  

       • The automated test will reject any deviation in spelling, spacing, or missing newlines.

    3. Create /home/user/archives/latest_manifest.log as a symbolic link that points to the newly generated manifest.

    4. Write a short policy note to /home/user/archives/README_BACKUPS.txt containing exactly these two lines (no extra spaces):

             RETENTION: 30 DAYS
             OWNER: backup-admin

       The file must end with a single newline.

    What will be verified
    ---------------------  
    • All three .tar.gz files exist under /home/user/archives/ with the correct names.  
    • Their contents really are compressed archives of the corresponding data_* directories.  
    • The manifest file exists, has precisely the format described above, and the size/check-sum values match the actual files.  
    • The symbolic link latest_manifest.log exists and resolves to the manifest.  
    • README_BACKUPS.txt exists with the two specified lines.  

    Remember: you must figure out the appropriate shell commands yourself; only the final state is checked.
truth:
Pre-existing items BEFORE the agent starts
    -----------------------------------------
    1. Directories  
       /home/user/data_alpha/  
       /home/user/data_bravo/  
       /home/user/data_charlie/

    2. Their exact file contents  
       /home/user/data_alpha/file1.txt   →  "alpha one\n"  
       /home/user/data_alpha/file2.txt   →  "alpha two\n"

       /home/user/data_bravo/file3.txt   →  "bravo three\n"  
       /home/user/data_bravo/file4.txt   →  "bravo four\n"

       /home/user/data_charlie/readme.md →  "# Charlie Data\nSome content.\n"

    Items the agent MUST create
    ---------------------------
    a) Directory:  /home/user/archives/    (0700 is enough; any user-writable mode accepted by tests)

    b) Files:
       /home/user/archives/data_alpha_20231001.tar.gz
       /home/user/archives/data_bravo_20231001.tar.gz
       /home/user/archives/data_charlie_20231001.tar.gz

       • The tests will untar each archive and verify that the file & directory hierarchy,
         modification times and payload match the original data_* directories.

    c) Manifest:
       /home/user/archives/backup_manifest_2023-10-01.log

       • Test routine:  
         1. Read manifest header and footer literally.  
         2. Expect 3 TAB-delimited records between them.  
         3. For every record:  
              – Check that the file name is one of the three required archives.  
              – Check that the size field equals `stat -c%s` of that archive.  
              – Check that the checksum field equals `sha256sum` of that archive.  
         4. Reject if any field count, order or delimiter differs.

    d) Symlink:
       /home/user/archives/latest_manifest.log  →  backup_manifest_2023-10-01.log  
       • Tests will confirm it is a symlink and its target basename matches exactly.

    e) Readme:
       /home/user/archives/README_BACKUPS.txt   (plain file, two lines, ending newline)  
       Contents exactly:
            RETENTION: 30 DAYS\n
            OWNER: backup-admin\n

    Permissions that will be accepted by the grader  
       • Directories: 700, 750 or 755  
       • Regular files: any readable mode (600 – 644)  
       • Symlink: mode ignored

    Additional grading notes  
       • The archives must be produced with GNU tar in the usual manner; the grader is
         insensitive to the user/group ownership stored inside the tarballs.  
       • Compression level is NOT checked, only that the output is valid gzip.  
       • Manifest file must end with a newline so that `wc -l` counts exactly 7 lines
         (header, blank, 3 archive lines, blank, footer).  
       • “TAB” means the ASCII 0x09 character, never spaces.  
       • All paths are case-sensitive and must not contain extra slashes.
